steps.title: Determine the annual multiplier
steps.content: When the value depreciates by 20% each year, 80% of the value remains. The multiplier per year is $1 - 0.20 = 0.8$.

steps.title: Calculate the 2-year depreciated value
steps.content: After 2 years, multiply the initial value by the multiplier twice: $$800 \times (0.8)^2 = 800 \times 0.64 = 512$$ This matches choice B — B is correct.
